event_claims.description: The go-shop period ran from the date of the merger agreement until 11:59 p.m. New York City time on May 7, 2015, during which the company, its subsidiaries and representatives were permitted to act directly or indirectly.
event_claims.quote_text: From the date of the merger agreement until 11:59 p.m. New York City time on May 7, 2015, which period we refer to as the "go-shop period," we and our subsidiaries and our respective representatives were permitted to, directly or indirectly:

event_claims.description: At the end of the go-shop period, the go-shop process ended.
event_claims.quote_text: At the end of the go-shop period:

event_claims.description: From 12:00 a.m. New York City time on May 8, 2015 until the effective time or earlier termination, the company, its affiliates and representatives were restricted from acting except as provided in the merger agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: Except as otherwise provided in the merger agreement, from and after 12:00 a.m. New York City time on May 8, 2015 until the effective time of the merger, or if earlier, the termination of the merger agreement in accordance with its terms, we and our affiliates and our respective representatives may not:

event_claims.description: Before the required stockholder vote, if the company receives an unsolicited acquisition proposal from a third party that constitutes or could reasonably be expected to lead to a superior proposal, the board may take specified actions subject to fiduciary-duty determinations and other conditions.
event_claims.quote_text: we receive an unsolicited acquisition proposal from a third party that in the good faith judgment of our board of directors, after consultation with outside legal and financial advisors, constitutes, or would reasonably be expected to lead to, a "superior proposal"

event_claims.description: The merger agreement allowed actions with an excluded party for so long as that person remained an excluded party.
event_claims.quote_text: taking actions with an excluded party (for so long as such person remains an excluded party)

event_claims.description: Before stockholder approval, subject to compliance and notice to Parent with an opportunity for Parent to revise the merger agreement, the board may take specified actions.
event_claims.quote_text:
Notwithstanding the foregoing restrictions, subject to our compliance with our obligations under the merger agreement, including providing Parent with advance notice and giving Parent an opportunity

to make revisions to the merger agreement, at any time prior to the approval and adoption of the merger agreement by our stockholders, our board of directors may:

event_claims.description: If the merger agreement is terminated by Parent after an adverse recommendation change or by the company to accept a superior proposal, the company must pay Parent a termination fee, with a reduced fee for specified go-shop or excluded-party superior proposals.
event_claims.quote_text: The merger agreement provides that, upon termination of the merger agreement by Parent as a result of an adverse recommendation change by our board of directors or by us to accept a superior proposal, we will be required to pay Parent a termination fee of (i) $8.75 million if such termination occurs during the go-shop period in respect of a superior proposal or if such termination is due to our acceptance of a superior proposal from an excluded party within a specified period of time after the go-shop period, or (ii) in all other circumstances, $17.5 million.
